A particular manufacturer packages amlodipine 2.5 mg tablets with an NDC of 00603-2108-02. If a pharmacy technician wants to receive hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 mg capsules from the same manufacturer, which of the following NDCs should they order?
A prescription medication that has passed its expiration date should be:

options.text: removed from active stock and set aside for disposal processing.
verified by the pharmacist so that it may be repackaged as a unit dose.
used to fill a prescription as soon as possible to minimize loss.
dispensed as usual for a period of no longer than 6 months.

stem: An expiration date provided by a manufacturer is 5/25. The last day this drug would be considered safely potent would be:
One agent that can be added to liquid preparations made from tablets to keep the medication uniformly suspended is:
Which of the following is an example of a medication in unit-of-use packaging?
Which of the following medications should be pulled from inventory due to incorrect storage?

options.text: Boostrix that was stored in a refrigerator
Havrix that was stored in a refrigerator
Shingrix that was stored in a freezer
Varivax that was stored in a freezer

stem: A vial of medication shows the manufacturer's expiration date as November 2026. The last date on which this medication may be used is:
A private company that serves pharmacies by returning certain unwanted or expired medications to the manufacturer is called a:
Which part of the meniscus should the pharmacy technician use to measure water in a graduated cylinder?
The primary purpose of rotating inventory in the pharmacy is to:

options.text: reduce the potential for dispensing expired medications.
help determine what needs to be placed on the next inventory order.
provide work for students and interns in the pharmacy.

stem: Which of the following needle gauges designates the largest bore?
A prescriber orders a medication for a patient with the following directions: Sig: i-ii tabs PO q4-6h p.r.n. pain. What is the appropriate translation for the prescription label?

options.text: Take 1 to 2 tablets by mouth every 6 hours as needed for pain
Take 1 to 2 tablets by mouth every 4 or 6 hours as needed
Take 1 to 2 tablets PO every 4 to 6 hours as needed for pain
Take 1 to 2 tablets by mouth every 4 to 6 hours as needed for pain

stem: Which of the following should be used to identify a particular batch of compounded product?
Hyperglycemia is a condition in which the patient has which of the following in their blood?
Which of the following products should be administered using a nebulizer?
A pharmacy receives the following prescription: Cephalexin 500 mg capsules, 1 cap PO q.i.d. #28. The prescription label should direct the patient to take 1 capsule by mouth:
What action should be taken to prevent an expired product from being used to fill prescriptions?

options.text: Segregate the product from regular inventory
Store the product with slow-selling items
Place the product at the back of the shelf
Place the product at the front of the shelf

stem: When verifying National Drug Codes on a received shipment, a pharmacy technician discovers that the supplier filled a purchase order for NDC 0093-3145-05 with NDC 0093-3107-05. The pharmacy technician realizes that the item received:

options.text: was made by a different manufacturer than the item on the purchase order.
is in a different package size than the item on the purchase order.
is generically equivalent to the item on the purchase order.
contains a different strength, form, or formulation than the item on the purchase order.

stem: A prescriber has ordered KCL 20 mEq PO daily for hypokalemia. The medication is supplied in 10 mEq/15 mL. How much, in mL, should be administered daily?
A dose of 1.5 fl oz is equivalent to:

The following order is written for acetaminophen with codeine elixir 120 mg/5 mL and 12 mg/5 mL, respectively: Sig: 1 tbsp PO q6h p.r.n., Disp: 4 oz. How much acetaminophen and codeine will each prescribed dose contain?
How much hydrocortisone, in mg, is contained in 1 oz of 2% hydrocortisone ointment?
A patient is to receive 2 tsp of a 5 mg/mL solution at bedtime, and b.i.d. p.r.n. A 480 mL stock bottle is dispensed. What is the minimum number of days this prescription will last?
A prescription is written for U-100 insulin to dispense 10 mL with directions to inject 40 units subcutaneously daily. How many days should the supply last if the medication is used as directed?
A patient injects 30 units of U-100 insulin every morning and 15 units every evening. How long, in days, should a 10 mL vial last the patient if taken as directed?
A physician writes a prescription for 1 gallon of sterile NS irrigation to be dispensed to a patient. The pharmacy stocks only 1000 mL bottles. How many bottles should be dispensed?
A prescription for a patient indicates a dosage of 1 fl oz t.i.d. for 5 days. What volume, in mL, should be dispensed?
What daily dose, in g, of a drug prescribed at 15 mg/kg/day should be administered to a patient weighing 65 kg?
How much acetylcysteine, in mg, is in 6 mL of acetylcysteine 20%?
If there are approximately 20 drops per mL in an eye solution, how long, in days, will 10 mL last if given 2 gtts OS b.i.d.?

The pharmacy receives the following prescription: Tamsulosin 0.4 mg caps, Sig: 0.4 mg PO daily for 2 weeks then increase to 0.8 mg daily thereafter. What quantity of capsules will be needed for the first 30 days?
A pharmacy technician receives a prescription for Biaxin 7.5 mg/kg every 12 hours for 10 days. The patient weighs 20 lb. How many mg should be dispensed per dose?
When 80 g of an ointment contains 4 g of zinc oxide, the percent strength of zinc oxide is:

A pharmacy receives an order for lactulose syrup 10 g/15 mL that reads, "Sig: 20 g PO q.h.s. Disp: 60 days supply." What should each dose of this prescription be, and what should be the total amount dispensed?
The pharmacy stocks a 300 mL bottle of 7.5% solution. If the dose of the solution is 1 tsp, what is the drug dose?
What volume, in mL, should a patient requiring 40 units of U-100 insulin receive?
How many methylphenidate 10 mg tablets are required to fill this prescription?

A prescription is written for 1 drop of a specified sodium fluoride solution daily; a volume of 24 mL is dispensed. If 1 mL of the solution provides 15 drops, for how many days will the patient's supply last?
If a patient is taking 0.5 g of cephalexin monohydrate t.i.d., the daily dose, in mg, is:
A 10 mL bottle of Humulin-N U-100 insulin is dispensed to a patient. If dosed at 35 units subcutaneously daily, for how long, in days, should this medication last?
How many 0.75 mg dexamethasone tablets are needed to administer a 3 mg dose?

A prescription for prednisone specifies dosing as 1 tsp q.i.d. for 3 days, then 1 tsp b.i.d. for 3 days, and finally 1 tsp daily for 3 days. Which of the following is the smallest bottle size, in fl oz, that can be used for the entire prescription?

A pharmacy receives a prescription for a 250 mg/5 mL suspension of cephalexin. The directions indicate that the patient should take 1.5 tsp three times a day. If the pharmacy stocks a 125 mg/5 mL suspension of cephalexin, the appropriate volume, in mL, per dose is:

A patient presents the following prescription: Humulin 70/30 U-100, Sig: 45 units subcutaneous q.a.m. and 55 units subcutaneous q.p.m. Disp: 30 days supply. The number of 10 mL vials to be entered into the patient profile for the quantity ordered is:
Given the following prescription: Hydrocortisone 1% ointment 15 g, Clotrimazole 1% cream 25 g, Petrolatum ointment q.s. ad 120 g. How much petrolatum ointment, in g, is needed for compounding?
What amount, in g, of active ingredient is in a 60 g tube of 2% triamcinolone cream?
A patient presents a prescription for Lovenox 60 mg b.i.d. for 2 weeks. How many doses should be dispensed to the patient?
A prescriber orders 1,000,000 units of penicillin G. The penicillin G on hand is available as a solution containing 250,000 units/mL. What volume of stock solution, in mL, is required?
If a child weighing 12 kg is to receive 40 mg/kg/dose of amoxicillin 400 mg/5 mL suspension, what should the volume of each dose be in mL?

The pharmacy receives a prescription for prednisone with the following directions: "Take 20 mg a day for the first three days, then decrease dose by 5 mg every three days until finished." How many 5 mg tablets should be dispensed to fill this prescription?
A pharmacy receives an order for amoxicillin suspension 200 mg to be taken every 8 hours. How many doses can be prepared from a 100 mL stock bottle of amoxicillin 250 mg/5 mL?

A patient brings in a prescription for amoxicillin 400 mg/5 mL suspension. The directions are to take 1 tsp PO q8h for 10 days. What volume, in mL, will the patient receive over the course of the 10 days?
A prescriber has written the following prescription: "Zithromax 100 mg/5 mL. Take 80 mg on day 1 and 40 mg once daily on days 2-5." The patient should:

options.text: take 1 tsp on day 1, then take 0.5 tsp once a day on days 2-5.
take 1 tsp on day 1, then take 2 mL once a day on days 2-5.
take 2 mL on day 1, then take 1 mL once a day on days 2-5.
take 4 mL on day 1, then take 2 mL once a day on days 2-5.

stem: What quantity, in mL, of 3% acetic acid must be mixed with 15% acetic acid to give 2500 mL of 10% acetic acid?
What quantity of neomycin is contained in 50 mL of a 1% neomycin solution?
Given the following prescription: Hydrocortisone 1.5%, Hydrophilic ointment q.s. ad 30 g. How many hydrocortisone 50 mg tablets are needed for dispensing?
How many rifampin 300 mg capsules are required to make a 240 mL stock solution of rifampin syrup 10 mg/mL?

A 4 oz volume of hydrocodone/chlorpheniramine suspension is dispensed to fill a prescription reading, "Sig: 1 tsp q12h p.r.n. cough." What is the minimum number of days that this medication should last?

The pharmacy receives the following prescription: Verapamil ER 120 mg, Disp: 30 tabs with 11 RF, Sig: 1 tab PO daily. The patient requests a 90 days supply of this medication. What quantity of tablets and refills could be dispensed with prescriber approval?
How many 45 g jars of 0.1% triamcinolone cream can be prepared from six 480 g jars?
Directions for reconstitution of an oral antibiotic suspension read: "Add 80 mL distilled water; each 5 mL contains 0.25 g." What is the dose of a prescription for 3/4 tsp?
The usual dose of pyrantel pamoate suspension for the treatment of pinworms is 11 mg/kg of body weight. How many mg of pyrantel pamoate should be given to a child weighing 88 lb?
If a pharmacy technician has drawn 50 units of U-100 insulin into a syringe, the plunger has been drawn to:
A 5 g suppository contains 5% phenylephrine and 20% zinc oxide as the active ingredients. How much zinc oxide, in g, is in the suppository?
A prescription is written for a tapering dose of prednisone 10 mg #42: Take 60 mg for 2 days and decrease by 10 mg every other day. What is the estimated days supply for this prescription?
A prescription reads, "Fentanyl transdermal patches 50 mcg/hr #10." The directions are to apply two patches and change them every 72 hours. How many days should be billed to the insurance?
Strong iodine solution containing 2.5 g/50 mL is prescribed at 0.3 mL t.i.d. How much iodine, in mg, should be administered daily?
How much active ingredient, in mg, is present in 3 mL of a 20% solution?
The physician gives the following order for a prescription: Cefaclor 250 mg/5mL, 1 tsp PO t.i.d. for 10 days. What is the amount, in mL, needed to fill the order?
How much of a drug prescribed at 5 mg/kg should be administered to a patient weighing 132 lbs?
A patient's prescription calls for cephalexin 125 mg/5 mL 1 tsp t.i.d. What volume, in mL, is the total daily dose?
A hospice patient requires lorazepam 1 mg PO q8h p.r.n. agitation. How many oral syringes can be prepared from two 30 mL lorazepam 2 mg/mL bottles?
A patient with epilepsy receives phenobarbital 55 mg PO b.i.d. How many mL should be administered per dose if the elixir provides 20 mg/5 mL?
A prescription is written for "2 tbsp q.i.d. (a.c. and h.s.)". What is the daily dose in mL?
What volume, in mL, of a medication available as a 75 mg/mL solution is required to deliver a dose of 65 mg?
A prescriber orders 1000 mg of a certain medication. If the label on the stock bottle indicates that each tablet contains 0.1 g, how many tablets should the patient receive?

Indomethacin liquid must be compounded according to the following prescription: Indomethacin 2 mg/mL, Cherry syrup q.s. ad 100 mL, Sig: 5 mL PO b.i.d. Only 50 mg capsules of indomethacin are available. How many capsules should be used to prepare this prescription?

A physician orders a 4.5 mcg/kg PO b.i.d. maintenance dose of digoxin pediatric elixir for an 11 lb baby. Each dose is administered with an oral syringe. What volume, in mL, of 0.05 mg/mL elixir should be drawn up in the syringe to deliver each dose?

A patient presents the following prescription: Humulin insulin U-100, Qty 10 mL vial, Inject 20 units subcutaneously in the morning and 30 units subcutaneously in the evening. How many vials should be dispensed for a 30 days supply?
A compound requires 200,000 units of nystatin powder. The powder is available at 690 units/mg. How much of the powder, in g, is needed to prepare the compound?
A prescription requires amoxicillin 250 mg/5 mL 2 tsp t.i.d. for 10 days. Which of the following is the most appropriate bottle size, in fluid oz, for the entire 10 days supply?
What volume, in mL, of Humulin R U-100 insulin is necessary to withdraw 28 units?
How much hydrocortisone powder, in g, is needed to compound 1 lb of 4% ointment?
A physician orders amoxicillin 50 mg/mL for a child at 1 mL PO q8h for 10 days. What is the total volume that must be dispensed, in mL, if the amoxicillin stock is 125 mg/5 mL?
If a prescription for aspirin 325 mg reads, "Sig: i tab PO q4-6h p.r.n.," what is the maximum number of tablets the patient may consume per day?
A cefaclor suspension label states, "Add 80 mL of water to yield 100 mL of 250 mg/5 mL." How much total water, in mL, is needed to yield 200 mL of 125 mg/5 mL?
A patient presents the following prescription: Norvasc 5 mg, 1 tab daily on even days, 2 tabs daily on odd days. Dispense a 1 month supply. How many tablets are needed to dispense a 30 days supply?

On 3/15/2025 a pharmacist asks a pharmacy technician to remove any medications from the pharmacy's shelves that have an expiration date fewer than 90 days from 3/15/2025. Which of the following products should be removed?

options.text: A bottle of amoxicillin 250 mg chewable tablets expiring 5/26
A refrigerated bottle of Humulin N expiring 12/25
A bottle of warfarin sodium 10 mg tablets expiring 6/25
A bottle of digoxin 0.125 mg expiring 5/25

stem: What volume, in mL, of a liquid suspension should be dispensed to administer a dose of 2 tbsp?
How many 2 tsp doses of cough syrup are contained in a 6 fl oz bottle?

A wholesale company's return policy allows the return of unopened stock containers for full credit as long as their expiration dates are at least 6 months away. If today's date is February 28, 2024, a pharmacy should be eligible to receive full credit for which of the following products?

options.text: A box of 10 enoxaparin 80 mg/0.8 mL syringes with an expiration date of April 30, 2024
A 3 mL bottle of moxifloxacin ophthalmic solution 0.5% with an expiration date of June 30, 2024
A 90 count bottle of rosuvastatin 20 mg with an expiration date of July 31, 2024
An 80 g tube of triamcinolone cream 0.1% with an expiration date of September 30, 2024

stem: How many 2 fl oz containers may be repackaged from a 3 L container?
A patient who weighs 110 lb requires an infusion of phenytoin at 5 mg/kg/dose t.i.d. How much phenytoin, in mg, should the patient receive daily?
How many 8 fl oz bottles can be packaged from a 1 gallon bottle of cough syrup?
Vials of 0.25 mg/5 mL Aloxi contain sufficient overfill to prepare 11 pre-filled syringes from 10 vials of drug. The number of pre-filled syringes that can be prepared from 50 vials of drug is:
If one tablespoon is prescribed q.i.d. (p.c. and h.s.) for 30 days, how many fluid oz should be dispensed to the patient?

A patient is to take 1.5 g of amoxicillin prior to a dental appointment. The capsules available in the pharmacy are amoxicillin 500 mg. What is the number of capsules that will be dispensed to the patient?
The following order arrives in the pharmacy: Lantus SoloStar #1 box (5 pens each containing 3 mL), Sig: 25 units SQ q.p.m. How long, in days, should this box of prefilled pens last the patient?
The stock solution of a medication contains 2 g/5 mL. What volume, in mL, is needed to deliver a dose of 100 mg?
If 200 mL of a 30% solution is diluted to a volume of 2 L, what is the resulting concentration?
A prescription reads: Zithromax 200 mg/5 mL q.s. 1.5 tsp on day 1, then 3/4 tsp on days 2-5. What volume, in mL, of Zithromax 200 mg/5 mL is needed for the entire course of therapy?
What volume, in mL, of 125 mg/5 mL amoxicillin suspension should be prepared to deliver a prescribed dose of 375 mg?
How many g of a drug are in 250 mL of a 40% solution?

A community pharmacy receives the following prescription: Meclizine 25 mg tabs #60, Sig: 1 tab PO q6h p.r.n. for vertigo. If the patient last received a dose of meclizine in an emergency department at 1:00 P.M., when may the next dose be administered if symptoms return?
An ear drop suspension contains 15 drops/mL. If the prescription reads, "Use 4 gtts AU h.s.", how many days should a 7.5 mL bottle last?
How many 4 mg syringes can be prepared from a 10 mL vial of midazolam 2 mg/2 mL injection?
A prescription is written for a Flovent HFA inhaler supplying 120 metered doses. The directions are 2 puffs b.i.d. What is the days supply?
A prescription is written for an albuterol HFA inhaler 18 g 2 puffs q.i.d. The inhaler supplies 200 metered doses. What is the days supply?
What amount of a drug, in mL, is required to prepare 2 pints of a 1:1300 solution?
A prescription order reads: Potassium chloride 10% (40 mEq/30 mL), Dispense: 240 mL, Sig: 1 tsp t.i.d. How many mEq of KCl are to be taken daily?
The following prescription is written for ProAir HFA: Sig: 2 puffs PO q.i.d. Dispense two inhalers of 8.5 g. How long, in days, should the supply last if there are 200 metered doses in each inhaler?
A prescription is written for cefdinir 125 mg/5 mL #60 mL "Sig: 150 mg PO b.i.d." How many days should the medication last?
A physician prescribes 5 mg of a drug per kg of body weight twice daily for a patient weighing 110 lb. How many 0.5 g tablets of the drug are required for a 10 day duration?
According to the following prescription order: Keflex 125 mg/5 mL, Sig: 1/2 tsp PO q.i.d. What dose, in mg, of Keflex should be administered daily?

The recommended starting dose of a chemotherapy agent is 20 mg/kg, calculated to the nearest whole tablet. It is available in 250 mg tablets. A patient weighing 162 lbs should receive a starting dose of how many tablets?
How long, in days, should it take for the patient to finish this medication?
How many syringes of enoxaparin 80 mg need to be dispensed for a 14 days supply if a patient weighs 165 lb and the dosage prescribed is 1 mg/kg q12h?
A prescription calls for a dose of 24 mg the first day, decreased by 4 mg daily on subsequent days until the dose reaches zero. How many 8 mg tablets are needed to fill the prescription?
What is the final concentration of a solution made by diluting a 4 oz bottle of a 15% solution to a final volume of 1 L?
What size bottle, in oz, is most appropriate to dispense 140 mL of ibuprofen suspension?

A pharmacy receives a prescription that reads: Prednisone 10 mg, Quantity: q.s. Take 3 tabs q.i.d. for 4 days, then 2 tabs t.i.d. for 3 days, then 1 tab t.i.d. for 2 days, then 1 tab b.i.d. for 2 days, then 1 tab q.d. for 2 days, then 1/2 tab q.d. for 2 days. How many 10 mg tablets must be dispensed to fill the prescription?
How many 1 oz bottles can be filled if 100 mL of a 30% acetic acid solution is diluted to 4%?

A pharmacy receives a new prescription that reads: Prednisone 5 mg, Sig: 30 mg PO day 1, 25 mg PO day 2, 20 mg PO day 3, 10 mg PO day 4, 5 mg PO day 5, 2.5 mg PO day 6. What is the correct number of tablets to be dispensed?
What volume, in mL, of 50% stock solution should be administered to a patient who is prescribed magnesium sulfate 2 g IV?
Amoxicillin is ordered at 40 mg/kg/day in 3 divided doses for a 15 lb infant. If the stock solution is amoxicillin 125 mg/5 mL, what volume, in mL, should be administered per dose?
How many fentanyl transdermal patches should be dispensed to a patient who is applying one patch every 72 hours for 2 weeks?

A patient whose body surface area is 1.78 m2 is to receive 5-fluorouracil 650 mg/m2/ day for 5 days. What is the total amount of medication, in g, this patient should receive during this course of therapy?

A patient is taking a medication prescribed by a physician at a dosing schedule of 2 tablets every 4 hours. If the patient is receiving 240 tablets at each fill, for how many days will the prescription supply last?
How many 1 oz jars can be filled with 500 g of a triple antibiotic ointment?

What volume, in mL, of a co-trimoxazole oral suspension containing trimethoprim 40 mg per teaspoon and sulfamethoxazole 200 mg per teaspoon should be repackaged in an oral syringe to equal co-trimoxazole double strength, containing trimethoprim 160 mg and sulfamethoxazole 800 mg?
The recommended dosage of a medication is 30 mg/kg b.i.d. The daily dose, in mg, for a 126 lb patient would be:
The directions for diluting a 0.25 g vial of a drug read, "Add 0.8 mL sterile water; each mL will contain 0.25 g." What volume, in mL, of the final solution will deliver a 500 mg dose?
A patient is to receive 30 mcg of a drug every day until a total cumulative dose of 1.5 mg is achieved. How many days will this therapy last?
The prescribed dose of Humulin R U-100 insulin is 10 units q.a.m. and 30 units q.p.m. a.c. How many 10 mL vials will be needed for a minimum of 30 days supply?
A physician writes a prescription for "lactulose syrup 1 tbsp PO b.i.d." What volume, in mL, should the patient take per day?
What volume, in mL, of digitoxin 0.2 mg/mL solution is required to deliver 0.03 mg of digitoxin?
A physician prescribes 210 mL of amoxicillin 250 mg/5 mL with the directions 2 tsp PO three times daily. What is the days supply of this prescription?
How much Mylanta, in mL, should be dispensed for the following prescription? Mylanta 1 oz q.i.d. (a.c. and h.s.) x 7 days.
A prescription for a child calls for amoxicillin suspension 150 mg q8h. The available amoxicillin suspension is 250 mg/5 mL. What volume, in mL, should the child receive daily?
What volume, in mL, of morphine sulfate that comes in 10 mL vials at a concentration of 5 mg/ mL will be needed to fill an order for 25 mg?
A chemotherapy regimen calls for cisplatin 75 mg/m2. The patient's weight is listed at 80 kg, and his body surface area (BSA) is 1.8 square meters. What is the proper dosage of cisplatin, in mg?
A prescription reads: Famotidine 40 mg/5 mL, 50 mL, Sig: 0.4 mL PO t.i.d. What amount of medication, in mg, is given each day?
The recommended child dose of cephalexin is 25 mg/kg/day in two divided doses. If the child weighs 44 lb, what is the correct individual dose?
A physician prescribes 5 mg of a drug per kg of body weight b.i.d. for a patient weighing 110 lb. How many 0.5 g tablets of the drug are required for a 10 day duration?
How many grams of dextrose are contained in 400 mL of 70% dextrose solution?
How much diphenhydramine elixir 12.5 mg/tsp is required, in mL, to fill the prescription, "Diphenhydramine 50 mg t.i.d. for 7 days"?
A patient's prescribed dose is 500 mg PO q.i.d. How many tablets are required to provide a 24 hour supply if the pharmacy stocks only 250 mg tablets?
How many total drops should be given in a day with the instructions, "ii gtt OU q.i.d."?
How many 0.75 mg tablets are needed to administer a 6 mg dose daily for 4 days?
How much magnesium sulfate 50%, in mL, is needed for a dose of 42 g?
A physician orders phenobarbital elixir 60 mg for a patient. The drug is available in a 20 mg/5 mL concentration. How many tsp should be given for each dose?
The pharmacy needs 300 mL of 25% dextrose. The pharmacy has 75% dextrose on hand. What volume of sterile water, in mL, will be needed to achieve the desired strength of 25%?

A pharmacy receives the following prescription: Warfarin 2.5 mg tablets, Disp: 4 weeks supply, Sig: 1 tab PO on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday, and 0.5 tab on Sunday, Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday. What quantity of tablets will be needed to fill this prescription if the first dose is taken on a Sunday?
A medication is ordered in a dose of 0.5 mg/kg/day. If the patient weighs 260 lb, what should the daily dose be, in mg?
How much neomycin, in g, is needed to make 1500 mL of a 0.25% solution for irrigation?
If 250 mL of 10% methyl salicylate in alcohol is diluted to 1000 mL, what will be the percentage strength of the final volume?
An order for a 1 mg atropine injection is filled using a vial labeled 0.4 mg/mL. What volume of the solution, in mL, should be administered?
